name: La Virgen del Llano a Cevico de la Torre a Vertavillo — circular desde Cubillas de Cerrato
description: This moderate 26.2-mile mountain biking route from Cubillas de Cerrato explores the varied landscapes of El Cerrato.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Cubillas De Cerrato traverse a varied natural environment within the Palencia Cerrato Plains, characterized by Castilian plains dotted with hills. The landscape includes farm fields and livestock areas, transitioning from vibrant greens in spring to golden tones in summer. The region also features the historic Canal de Castilla, offering scenic routes along its banks. This diverse terrain provides a range of experiences for mountain biking enthusiasts. Best mountain bike trails around Cubillas De Cerrato The most popular mountain bike trail is Monte de la Villa Viewpoints Trail, a 21.7 miles (35.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores varied landscapes and offers scenic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Virgen del Llano a Cevico de la Torre a Vertavillo — circular desde Cubillas de Cerrato, a moderate 26.1 miles (42.1 km) path. This route winds through the plains and hills of El Cerrato. Local mountain bikers also love the Puente Mayor de Palencia – Shelter loop from Dueñas, a 31.4 miles (50.5 km) trail leading through the banks of the Canal de Castilla, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Cubillas De Cerrato is defined by Castilian plains, rolling hills, and the Canal de Castilla. The network offers options for various ability levels, with routes ranging from easy to moderate. The routes in Cubillas De Cerrato are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.15 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cubillas De Cerrato's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cubillas De Cerrato?
A: There are 16 mountain bike trails around Cubillas De Cerrato, offering a variety of experiences for riders. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.15 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cubillas De Cerrato?
A: The terrain around Cubillas De Cerrato is diverse, characterized by the Castilian plains dotted with hills, farm fields, and livestock areas. You'll find routes winding through these varied landscapes, with some trails featuring 'chunky' sections, rock gardens, and slab rolls for a more rigorous experience. The historic Canal de Castilla also offers scenic, flatter routes along its banks.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cubillas De Cerrato?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy mountain bike trails available in Cubillas De Cerrato, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. The region generally offers options for various ability levels, with routes ranging from easy to moderate.

Q: What are some options for more experienced mountain bikers in the area?
A: For more experienced mountain bikers, there are 14 moderate trails that offer a good challenge. Some routes feature 'chunky' terrain with chutes, rock gardens, and slab rolls. Consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324118909/la-virgen-del-llano-a-cevico-de-la-torre-a-vertavillo-circular-desde-cubillas-de-cerrato" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Virgen del Llano a Cevico de la Torre a Vertavillo — circular desde Cubillas de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through the plains and hills of El Cerrato, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia – Shelter loop from Dueñas&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you along the Canal de Castilla.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Cubillas De Cerrato?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324118909/la-virgen-del-llano-a-cevico-de-la-torre-a-vertavillo-circular-desde-cubillas-de-cerrato" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Virgen del Llano a Cevico de la Torre a Vertavillo — circular desde Cubillas de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 26.1-mile path. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia – Shelter loop from Dueñas&lt;/a&gt;, a 31.4-mile trail along the Canal de Castilla.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while mountain biking around Cubillas De Cerrato?
A: The region offers several points of interest. You can cycle along the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4270308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Along the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, an 18th-century engineering marvel. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6158493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Botijo Monument of Dueñas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Dueñas&lt;/a&gt;. The Roman Villa of La Olmeda is also a significant archaeological treasure a few kilometers away.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cubillas De Cerrato?
A: The landscape transforms with the seasons. Spring is vibrant with green fields and red poppies, making it a beautiful time to ride. Summer brings golden tones to the plains. While specific weather patterns aren't detailed, generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities, avoiding the potential heat of mid-summer.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain biking options in Cubillas De Cerrato?
A: Yes, with 2 easy trails available, families can find suitable routes for a more relaxed biking experience. The scenic routes along the Canal de Castilla, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31460694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Botijo Monument of Dueñas – Lock 33, Canal de Castilla loop from Dueñas&lt;/a&gt;, are generally flatter and can be enjoyable for families.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Cubillas De Cerrato?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.15 stars from over 9 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied natural environment, the mix of Castilian plains and rolling hills, and the scenic routes along the Canal de Castilla. The diversity of terrain offers something for different skill levels.

Q: How long do mountain bike routes typically take to complete in Cubillas De Cerrato?
A: The duration of routes varies depending on their length and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159513093/monte-de-la-villa-viewpoints-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de la Villa Viewpoints Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 21.7 miles (35.0 km), typically takes about 2 hours 51 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31467740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia – Shelter loop from Dueñas&lt;/a&gt; (31.4 miles / 50.5 km) can take around 3 hours 51 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific scenic viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159513093/monte-de-la-villa-viewpoints-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de la Villa Viewpoints Trail&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for offering scenic views. The region's characteristic Castilian plains dotted with hills also provide numerous opportunities for expansive vistas, especially during spring with its vibrant colors or summer with its golden tones.
tips: This small town sits on the side of a small mountain, El Castillo, and overlooks the plains below. The Church of Santa María la Mayor is located at the highest point of the town.
Here you will find the Hermitage of the Virgen del Llano with a beautiful park around it. There is a playground and a picnic area. There are also great views of the surrounding area from here.
Vertavillo is a charming municipality in the province of Palencia, Castilla y León. This village, located in the Cerrato region, offers a rich history and an impressive natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the La Virgen del Llano a Cevico de la Torre a Vertavillo — circular desde Cubillas de Cerrato mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate mountain bike ride. Riders should have good fitness and advanced riding skills, aligning with approximately an STS S2 technical difficulty rating. It's a solid challenge for those with some experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied landscapes typical of the El Cerrato region. You'll encounter dirt trails and paths, including what are sometimes called 'gravel highways.' The grip levels can differ based on the earth type. While some sections, suggested by 'Virgen del Llano' (Virgin of the Plain), might be flatter or more open, you should also expect undulating or hilly terrain characteristic of a 'Cerrato' area.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the circular route passes by several interesting spots. You'll start near Cubillas de Cerrato Village. Further along, you can see the Ermita de la Virgen del Llano, and towards the end, the Church of San Miguel Arcángel and Rollo de Vertavillo.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Cubillas de Cerrato?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Cubillas de Cerrato is the starting point for this circular route. You should be able to find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of the village, which serves as the trailhead. Look for public parking spaces or areas designated for visitors.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a 42 km ride and bring plenty of water for both of you.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The El Cerrato region experiences hot summers and cold winters. The best times to ride this trail are typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-November). During these seasons, the weather is usually milder and more pleasant for mountain biking, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to ride this mountain bike trail. It's a public route accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near several villages, including Cubillas de Cerrato, Cevico de la Torre, and Vertavillo. You should be able to find local cafes or small restaurants in these villages to refuel before, during, or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and 42 km distance, you should pack essentials like plenty of water, energy snacks, a basic bike repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for the weather. A helmet is mandatory, and sunglasses are recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any alternative or connecting trails in the Vertavillo area?
faqs.answer: While this specific route is a circular one, the broader El Cerrato Palentino region is known for its network of gravel and dirt trails. You might find other local paths around Vertavillo if you're looking to extend your ride or explore different routes. This trail itself briefly uses sections of the Colada y Camino de Valdelázaro and Camino a la Finca Doncel.
